title: Pilosella nydeggerorum
taxonomy: Pilosella nydeggerorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pilosella, which consists of dandelion-like plants. The genus Pilosella is often divided into several subgenera, with P. nydeggerorum being part of the subgenus Eunomius. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flower structure. Pilosella nydeggerorum is native to the mountainous regions of Central Europe.

intro: Pilosella nydeggerorum, also known as Nydegger's mouse-ear hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to support immune function and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella nydeggerorum include sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as other compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids.
